title: Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte ‘interested’ in a trade, NFL insider says
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots wide receiver Kayshon Boutte is reportedly interested in a trade, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.
Boutte has missed the first two Patriots' open OTA sessions and is entering the final year of his rookie contract.
Last season, Boutte was second on the team in receiving touchdowns and played 55% of offensive snaps.

title: Visiting Boston this summer? Here are 8 navigation tips you need to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston prepares for major summer events including FIFA World Cup, Tall Ships, and America’s 250th anniversary celebrations.
Rideshare pickups at Logan Airport are limited to designated areas; taxis are available at terminal arrival levels.
Public transportation options include MBTA trains, buses, ferries, and special services for events like World Cup matches.

title: Rent control proponents offer compromise with local option instead of statewide cap
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts rent control proponents offer a compromise to allow municipalities to adopt local rent control instead of a statewide cap.
The compromise proposal caps annual rent hikes at 10 percent and allows for rent increases between tenancies, banning no-fault evictions.
Opponents, including Housing for Massachusetts, have not agreed to the compromise, expressing concerns about housing market impacts.

title: Report: Patriots ‘aware’ of A.J. Brown’s degenerative knee condition before trade, Eliot Wolf says
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots traded for A.J. Brown, aware of his degenerative knee condition, giving up a 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round pick.
A.J. Brown passed a physical and reassured reporters he is not concerned about his knee despite other teams' hesitations.
Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler, has a history of durability, having played in at least 13 games every NFL season.

title: Report: Giannis Antetokounmpo is intrigued by idea of joining ‘wild card’ Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is reportedly intrigued by joining the Boston Celtics, according to The Athletic's Sam Amick.
While no direct talks have occurred, the Celtics are considered a potential 'wild card' for acquiring Antetokounmpo.
Antetokounmpo's current contract includes one year left and a player option, influencing trade considerations.

title: E-bicyclist, 14, injured in collision with vehicle in Medway
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 14-year-old e-bicyclist was seriously injured in a collision with a passenger vehicle in Medway, Massachusetts.
The incident occurred at the intersection of Village and Oakland streets; injuries were significant but not life-threatening.
Boston MedFlight transported the teenager to a regional trauma center after the crash on Wednesday morning.

title: Massachusetts tops WalletHub ranking of state economies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts ranked first in WalletHub's state economy rankings, outperforming Washington and Utah.
State excelled due to high-tech job share, research investment, and high invention patents per capita.
New Hampshire ranked 13th, Connecticut 16th, Vermont 29th, Rhode Island 47th, and Maine 48th in economic performance.

title: Turner or Freese? Brad Friedel has a unique perspective on the U.S. World Cup goalkeeper debate
author: Seth Macomber
bullets: Brad Friedel discusses the U.S. men's national team goalkeeper options for the 2026 World Cup: Matt Turner and Matt Freese.
Turner has strong shot-stopping abilities but is critiqued for footwork, while Freese excels in dealing with crosses.
Both Turner and Freese developed later, highlighting issues in U.S. Soccer's past goalkeeper training focus.

title: Review & setlist: A$AP Rocky turns TD Garden into a massive moshpit
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A$AP Rocky turned TD Garden into a moshpit with his high-energy 'Don't Be Dumb' World Tour stop.
Rocky performed over 30 songs, mixing tracks from his new album with fan favorites, amid technical issues.
Guest appearances included A$AP Twelvyy, Tommy Revenge, and Thoto, enhancing the concert's visual and energetic performance.

title: Julian Edelman reacts to Patriots’ A.J. Brown trade: ‘Everybody’s job becomes easier’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots trade a 2028 first-round pick to acquire wide receiver A.J. Brown from the Philadelphia Eagles.
Julian Edelman believes A.J. Brown's presence will ease pressures on other Patriots receivers, enhancing overall team performance.
Josh McDaniels and Todd Downing praise A.J. Brown's strength, competitiveness, and improvements in his game understanding.
